attributes.description: Forensic pack for process execution and Amcache analysis — prefetch, shimcache, process listing, suspicious process detection across all platforms, plus Windows Amcache application inventory, driver forensics, shortcuts, device PnP, and enriched views.
attributes.name: forensic-process-execution

queries.query:
-- Windows Prefetch Execution Artifacts Analysis
-- Source: Native osquery table (prefetch)
-- Parses .pf files to extract executable names, run counts, timestamps, and accessed resources
-- Focus: Evidence of program execution history for forensic investigation (malware, lateral movement tools, deleted executables)
SELECT
  datetime(last_run_time, 'unixepoch') AS last_execution,
  filename,
  hash,
  run_count,
  other_run_times,
  size,
  volume_serial,
  datetime(volume_creation, 'unixepoch') AS volume_creation_time,
  accessed_files_count,
  accessed_directories_count,
  accessed_files,
  accessed_directories,
  path AS prefetch_file_path
FROM prefetch
WHERE last_run_time > (strftime('%s', 'now') - 7776000)
  AND filename NOT LIKE 'SEARCHINDEXER%'
  AND filename NOT LIKE 'SEARCHPROTOCOLHOST%'
  AND filename NOT LIKE 'SEARCHFILTERHOST%'
  AND filename NOT LIKE 'RUNTIMEBROKER%'
  AND filename NOT LIKE 'BACKGROUNDTASKHOST%'
  AND filename NOT LIKE 'WMIPRVSE%'
  AND filename NOT LIKE 'DLLHOST%'
  AND filename NOT LIKE 'TASKHOSTW%'
ORDER BY last_run_time DESC

-- Application Compatibility Cache (ShimCache) Execution Tracking
-- Source: Native osquery table (shimcache)
-- Focus: Suspicious program execution from user-writable staging locations

WITH extracted AS (
  SELECT
    s.entry,
    s.path,
    datetime(s.modified_time, 'unixepoch') AS modified_time,
    s.execution_flag,
    h.md5,
    h.sha256,
    concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/', h.sha256) AS vt_link,
    a.subject_name AS signature_signer,
    a.result AS signature_status
  FROM shimcache s
  LEFT JOIN hash h ON h.path = s.path
  LEFT JOIN authenticode a ON a.path = s.path
  WHERE
    -- Exclude legitimate Windows system directories (protected paths)
    s.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\%'
    AND s.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\%'
    AND s.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\%'
    AND s.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\servicing\\%'
    AND s.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\%'
    AND s.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\Windows NT\\%'
    AND s.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\%'
)

SELECT *
FROM extracted
WHERE (
  -- Condition 1: Unsigned binaries (no code signature) - always suspicious
  signature_signer IS NULL
  OR signature_status IS NULL
  -- Condition 2: Invalid or untrusted signatures (osquery returns 'trusted' for valid)
  OR signature_status NOT IN ('trusted', 'valid')
  -- Condition 3: Execution from high-risk user-writable staging locations
  OR regex_match(path, '\\\\Users\\\\[^\\\\]+\\\\(Downloads|Desktop|Documents)\\\\|\\\\Users\\\\[^\\\\]+\\\\AppData\\\\(Local\\\\Temp|Roaming)\\\\|\\\\Windows\\\\Temp\\\\|\\\\ProgramData\\\\[^\\\\]+\\\\|^C:\\\\[^\\\\]+\\.exe$|\\\\(Temp|tmp)\\\\', 0) IS NOT NULL
)
-- Exclude trusted Microsoft-signed binaries to reduce noise
AND NOT (
  signature_signer LIKE '%Microsoft%'
  AND signature_status = 'trusted'
)
ORDER BY entry ASC;

-- Windows Process Listing with Full Forensic Context
-- Provides comprehensive process information including parent chain, hashes, code signatures, and username
-- Use for: Threat hunting, incident response, baseline analysis
SELECT
    p.pid,
    p.name,
    p.path,
    p.cmdline,
    p.cwd,
    p.parent AS ppid,
    pp.name AS parent_name,
    pp.path AS parent_path,
    pp.cmdline AS parent_cmdline,
    p.uid,
    u.username,
    p.state,
    datetime(p.start_time, 'unixepoch') AS start_time,
    p.resident_size,
    p.total_size,
    p.threads,
    p.handle_count,
    p.on_disk,
    p.elevated_token,
    h.md5,
    h.sha1,
    h.sha256,
    CASE
        WHEN h.sha256 IS NOT NULL AND h.sha256 != '' THEN concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/', h.sha256)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS vt_link,
    a.result AS signature_status,
    a.subject_name AS signature_signer
FROM processes p
LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
LEFT JOIN users u ON p.uid = u.uid
LEFT JOIN hash h ON p.path = h.path
LEFT JOIN authenticode a ON p.path = a.path;

-- Linux Process Listing with Full Forensic Context
-- Provides comprehensive process information including parent chain, hashes, and username
-- Use for: Threat hunting, incident response, baseline analysis
SELECT
    p.pid,
    p.name,
    p.path,
    p.cmdline,
    p.cwd,
    p.parent AS ppid,
    pp.name AS parent_name,
    pp.path AS parent_path,
    pp.cmdline AS parent_cmdline,
    p.uid,
    u.username,
    p.gid,
    p.euid,
    p.egid,
    p.state,
    datetime(p.start_time, 'unixepoch') AS start_time,
    p.resident_size,
    p.total_size,
    p.threads,
    p.nice,
    p.on_disk,
    h.md5,
    h.sha1,
    h.sha256,
    CASE
        WHEN h.sha256 IS NOT NULL AND h.sha256 != '' THEN concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/', h.sha256)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS vt_link
FROM processes p
LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
LEFT JOIN users u ON p.uid = u.uid
LEFT JOIN hash h ON p.path = h.path;

-- macOS Process Listing with Full Forensic Context
-- Provides comprehensive process information including parent chain, hashes, code signatures, and username
-- Use for: Threat hunting, incident response, baseline analysis
SELECT
    p.pid,
    p.name,
    p.path,
    p.cmdline,
    p.cwd,
    p.parent AS ppid,
    pp.name AS parent_name,
    pp.path AS parent_path,
    pp.cmdline AS parent_cmdline,
    p.uid,
    u.username,
    p.gid,
    p.euid,
    p.egid,
    p.state,
    datetime(p.start_time, 'unixepoch') AS start_time,
    p.resident_size,
    p.total_size,
    p.threads,
    p.nice,
    p.on_disk,
    h.md5,
    h.sha1,
    h.sha256,
    CASE
        WHEN h.sha256 IS NOT NULL AND h.sha256 != '' THEN concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/', h.sha256)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS vt_link,
    CASE
        WHEN s.signed = 1 THEN 'signed'
        WHEN s.signed = 0 THEN 'unsigned'
        ELSE 'unknown'
    END AS signature_status,
    s.authority AS signature_signer,
    s.identifier AS bundle_identifier,
    s.team_identifier
FROM processes p
LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
LEFT JOIN users u ON p.uid = u.uid
LEFT JOIN hash h ON p.path = h.path
LEFT JOIN signature s ON p.path = s.path;

-- Windows Suspicious Process Detection
-- Identifies processes with potentially malicious characteristics
SELECT
    p.pid,
    p.name,
    p.path,
    p.cmdline,
    p.cwd,
    p.parent AS ppid,
    pp.name AS parent_name,
    pp.path AS parent_path,
    pp.cmdline AS parent_cmdline,
    p.uid,
    p.state,
    datetime(p.start_time, 'unixepoch') AS start_time,
    p.on_disk,
    p.elevated_token,
    h.md5,
    h.sha1,
    h.sha256,
    CASE
        WHEN h.sha256 IS NOT NULL AND h.sha256 != '' THEN concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/', h.sha256)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS vt_link,
    a.result AS signature_status,
    a.subject_name AS signature_signer,
    CASE
        WHEN a.result IS NOT NULL AND a.result != 'trusted' THEN 'untrusted_signature'
        WHEN a.result IS NULL AND p.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\%' THEN 'unsigned_non_system'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '%\\Temp\\%' OR p.path LIKE '%\\tmp\\%' THEN 'suspicious_path_temp'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' THEN 'suspicious_path_appdata_temp'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '%\\Users\\Public\\%' THEN 'suspicious_path_public'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '%\\Downloads\\%' THEN 'suspicious_path_downloads'
        WHEN p.name IN ('powershell.exe', 'pwsh.exe') AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%encodedcommand%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%-e %' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%-enc %' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%bypass%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%hidden%') THEN 'powershell_suspicious_args'
        WHEN p.name = 'cmd.exe' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%/c %' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%/k %') AND pp.name NOT IN ('explorer.exe', 'cmd.exe', 'powershell.exe', 'pwsh.exe') THEN 'cmd_unusual_parent'
        WHEN p.name IN ('mshta.exe', 'wscript.exe', 'cscript.exe') THEN 'script_host_execution'
        WHEN p.name IN ('certutil.exe', 'bitsadmin.exe') AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%http%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%ftp%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%decode%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%urlcache%') THEN 'lolbin_download'
        WHEN p.name = 'rundll32.exe' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%javascript%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%vbscript%') THEN 'rundll32_script'
        WHEN p.name IN ('regsvr32.exe', 'msiexec.exe') AND p.cmdline LIKE '%http%' THEN 'lolbin_remote_payload'
        WHEN p.name IN ('msbuild.exe', 'installutil.exe', 'regasm.exe', 'regsvcs.exe') THEN 'dotnet_lolbin'
        WHEN p.name IN ('eventvwr.exe', 'fodhelper.exe', 'computerdefaults.exe', 'sdclt.exe') AND pp.name NOT IN ('explorer.exe', 'svchost.exe') THEN 'uac_bypass_tool'
        WHEN p.name = 'wmic.exe' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%process%call%create%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%/node:%') THEN 'wmic_execution'
        WHEN pp.name = 'wmiprvse.exe' OR pp.name = 'wsmprovhost.exe' THEN 'remote_execution_parent'
        WHEN p.on_disk = 0 THEN 'process_not_on_disk'
        ELSE 'other_suspicious'
    END AS detection_reason
FROM processes p
LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
LEFT JOIN hash h ON p.path = h.path
LEFT JOIN authenticode a ON p.path = a.path
WHERE p.path != ''
AND p.name NOT IN ('Registry', 'Memory Compression')
AND (
    -- Untrusted signatures (explicit non-trusted result)
    (a.result IS NOT NULL AND a.result != 'trusted')
    -- Unsigned non-system binaries
    OR (a.result IS NULL AND p.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\%' AND p.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\%' AND p.path NOT LIKE 'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\%')
    -- Suspicious execution paths (narrowed to reduce noise)
    OR p.path LIKE '%\\Temp\\%'
    OR p.path LIKE '%\\tmp\\%'
    OR p.path LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%'
    OR p.path LIKE '%\\Users\\Public\\%'
    OR p.path LIKE '%\\Downloads\\%'
    -- PowerShell abuse patterns
    OR (p.name IN ('powershell.exe', 'pwsh.exe') AND (
        p.cmdline LIKE '%encodedcommand%'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%-e %'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%-enc %'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%bypass%'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%hidden%'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%downloadstring%'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%iex%'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%invoke-expression%'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%webclient%'
    ))
    -- Script host execution
    OR p.name IN ('mshta.exe', 'wscript.exe', 'cscript.exe')
    -- LOLBin download/decode
    OR (p.name IN ('certutil.exe', 'bitsadmin.exe') AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%http%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%decode%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%urlcache%'))
    -- Rundll32 script execution
    OR (p.name = 'rundll32.exe' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%javascript%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%vbscript%'))
    -- Remote payload LOLBins
    OR (p.name IN ('regsvr32.exe', 'msiexec.exe') AND p.cmdline LIKE '%http%')
    -- .NET LOLBins (AppLocker bypass)
    OR p.name IN ('msbuild.exe', 'installutil.exe', 'regasm.exe', 'regsvcs.exe')
    -- UAC bypass tools with unusual parents
    OR (p.name IN ('eventvwr.exe', 'fodhelper.exe', 'computerdefaults.exe', 'sdclt.exe') AND pp.name NOT IN ('explorer.exe', 'svchost.exe'))
    -- WMIC lateral movement / execution
    OR (p.name = 'wmic.exe' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%process%call%create%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%/node:%'))
    -- Remote execution indicators
    OR pp.name IN ('wmiprvse.exe', 'wsmprovhost.exe')
    -- Process running from memory (not on disk)
    OR p.on_disk = 0
);

-- Linux Suspicious Process Detection
-- Identifies processes with potentially malicious characteristics
SELECT
    p.pid,
    p.name,
    p.path,
    p.cmdline,
    p.cwd,
    p.parent AS ppid,
    pp.name AS parent_name,
    pp.path AS parent_path,
    pp.cmdline AS parent_cmdline,
    p.uid,
    p.gid,
    p.euid,
    p.egid,
    p.state,
    datetime(p.start_time, 'unixepoch') AS start_time,
    p.on_disk,
    h.md5,
    h.sha1,
    h.sha256,
    CASE
        WHEN h.sha256 IS NOT NULL AND h.sha256 != '' THEN concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/', h.sha256)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS vt_link,
    CASE
        WHEN p.path LIKE '/tmp/%' OR p.path LIKE '/var/tmp/%' THEN 'suspicious_path_tmp'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '/dev/shm/%' THEN 'suspicious_path_devshm'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '/home/%/.%' THEN 'hidden_in_home'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%/dev/tcp/%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%/dev/udp/%' THEN 'reverse_shell_devtcp'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%nc %' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%-e %' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%-c %') THEN 'netcat_shell'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%ncat %' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%-e %' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%-c %') THEN 'ncat_shell'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%bash -i%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%sh -i%' THEN 'interactive_shell'
        WHEN p.name IN ('python', 'python3', 'perl', 'ruby', 'php') AND p.cmdline LIKE '%socket%' THEN 'script_socket'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%curl%|%sh%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%wget%|%sh%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%curl%|%bash%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%wget%|%bash%' THEN 'download_and_execute'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%base64 -d%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%base64 --decode%' THEN 'base64_decode'
        WHEN p.name LIKE '.%' THEN 'hidden_process_name'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%stratum%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%xmr%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%monero%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%nicehash%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%pool.%' THEN 'crypto_miner'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%nsenter%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%--target 1%' THEN 'container_escape'
        WHEN p.uid = 0 AND p.path NOT LIKE '/usr/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/sbin/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/bin/%' THEN 'root_unusual_path'
        WHEN p.on_disk = 0 THEN 'process_not_on_disk'
        ELSE 'other_suspicious'
    END AS detection_reason
FROM processes p
LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
LEFT JOIN hash h ON p.path = h.path
WHERE p.path != ''
AND (
    -- Suspicious execution paths
    p.path LIKE '/tmp/%'
    OR p.path LIKE '/var/tmp/%'
    OR p.path LIKE '/dev/shm/%'
    OR p.path LIKE '/home/%/.%'
    -- Reverse shell patterns
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%/dev/tcp/%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%/dev/udp/%'
    -- Netcat reverse shells
    OR (p.cmdline LIKE '%nc %' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%-e %' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%-c %'))
    OR (p.cmdline LIKE '%ncat %' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%-e %' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%-c %'))
    OR (p.cmdline LIKE '%socat %' AND p.cmdline LIKE '%exec%')
    -- Interactive shell spawning
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%bash -i%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%sh -i%'
    -- Script interpreters with network activity
    OR (p.name IN ('python', 'python3', 'python2', 'perl', 'ruby', 'php') AND p.cmdline LIKE '%socket%')
    -- Download and execute patterns
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%curl%|%sh%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%wget%|%sh%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%curl%|%bash%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%wget%|%bash%'
    -- Base64 decode (often used in obfuscation)
    OR (p.cmdline LIKE '%base64%' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%-d%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%--decode%'))
    -- Hidden process names
    OR p.name LIKE '.%'
    -- Running from memory
    OR p.on_disk = 0
    -- Crypto-miner indicators
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%stratum%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%xmr%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%monero%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%nicehash%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%pool.%mining%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%cryptonight%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%randomx%'
    -- Container escape indicators
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%nsenter%'
    OR (p.cmdline LIKE '%--target%' AND p.cmdline LIKE '%--mount%')
    OR (p.cmdline LIKE '%--target 1%')
    -- Root processes from unusual locations
    OR (p.uid = 0 AND p.path NOT LIKE '/usr/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/sbin/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/bin/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/opt/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/lib/%')
);

-- macOS Suspicious Process Detection
-- Identifies processes with potentially malicious characteristics
SELECT
    p.pid,
    p.name,
    p.path,
    p.cmdline,
    p.cwd,
    p.parent AS ppid,
    pp.name AS parent_name,
    pp.path AS parent_path,
    pp.cmdline AS parent_cmdline,
    p.uid,
    p.gid,
    p.euid,
    p.egid,
    p.state,
    datetime(p.start_time, 'unixepoch') AS start_time,
    p.on_disk,
    h.md5,
    h.sha1,
    h.sha256,
    CASE
        WHEN h.sha256 IS NOT NULL AND h.sha256 != '' THEN concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/', h.sha256)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS vt_link,
    CASE
        WHEN s.signed = 1 THEN 'signed'
        WHEN s.signed = 0 THEN 'unsigned'
        ELSE 'unknown'
    END AS signature_status,
    s.authority AS signature_signer,
    s.identifier AS bundle_identifier,
    s.team_identifier,
    CASE
        WHEN s.signed = 0 OR s.signed IS NULL THEN 'unsigned_binary'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '/tmp/%' OR p.path LIKE '/var/tmp/%' THEN 'suspicious_path_tmp'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '/private/tmp/%' THEN 'suspicious_path_private_tmp'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '/Users/Shared/%' THEN 'suspicious_path_shared'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '%/.%' THEN 'hidden_path'
        WHEN p.path LIKE '/Users/%/Library/%' AND s.signed = 0 THEN 'unsigned_in_library'
        WHEN p.name = 'osascript' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%JavaScript%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%do shell script%') THEN 'osascript_abuse'
        WHEN p.name = 'curl' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%|%sh%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%|%bash%') THEN 'curl_pipe_shell'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%xattr -d com.apple.quarantine%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%xattr -c%' THEN 'quarantine_removal'
        WHEN p.name IN ('python', 'python3', 'perl', 'ruby') AND p.cmdline LIKE '%socket%' THEN 'script_socket'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%base64%' AND p.cmdline LIKE '%-D%' THEN 'base64_decode'
        WHEN p.name LIKE '.%' THEN 'hidden_process_name'
        WHEN p.cmdline LIKE '%stratum%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%xmr%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%monero%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%nicehash%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%pool.%' THEN 'crypto_miner'
        WHEN p.uid = 0 AND p.path NOT LIKE '/usr/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/sbin/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/bin/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/System/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/Applications/%' THEN 'root_unusual_path'
        WHEN p.on_disk = 0 THEN 'process_not_on_disk'
        ELSE 'other_suspicious'
    END AS detection_reason
FROM processes p
LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
LEFT JOIN hash h ON p.path = h.path
LEFT JOIN signature s ON p.path = s.path
WHERE p.path != ''
AND (
    -- Unsigned or ad-hoc signed binaries
    s.signed = 0 OR s.signed IS NULL
    -- Suspicious execution paths
    OR p.path LIKE '/tmp/%'
    OR p.path LIKE '/var/tmp/%'
    OR p.path LIKE '/private/tmp/%'
    OR p.path LIKE '/Users/Shared/%'
    OR p.path LIKE '%/.%'
    -- osascript abuse (AppleScript for command execution)
    OR (p.name = 'osascript' AND (
        p.cmdline LIKE '%JavaScript%'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%do shell script%'
        OR p.cmdline LIKE '%NSAppleScript%'
    ))
    -- Download and execute patterns
    OR (p.name = 'curl' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%|%sh%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%|%bash%'))
    OR (p.name = 'wget' AND (p.cmdline LIKE '%|%sh%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%|%bash%'))
    -- Quarantine attribute removal (Gatekeeper bypass)
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%xattr -d com.apple.quarantine%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%xattr -c%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%xattr -r -d%'
    -- Script interpreters with network activity
    OR (p.name IN ('python', 'python3', 'python2', 'perl', 'ruby', 'php') AND p.cmdline LIKE '%socket%')
    -- Base64 decode (obfuscation)
    OR (p.cmdline LIKE '%base64%' AND p.cmdline LIKE '%-D%')
    -- Hidden process names
    OR p.name LIKE '.%'
    -- Running from memory
    OR p.on_disk = 0
    -- Crypto-miner indicators (T1496 Resource Hijacking)
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%stratum%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%xmr%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%monero%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%nicehash%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%pool.%mining%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%cryptonight%'
    OR p.cmdline LIKE '%randomx%'
    -- Root processes from unusual locations
    OR (p.uid = 0 AND p.path NOT LIKE '/usr/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/sbin/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/bin/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/System/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/Applications/%' AND p.path NOT LIKE '/Library/%')
);

-- Amcache Application Forensics: Combined application and file inventory
-- Source: elastic_amcache_applications_view (Amcache.hve registry hive)
-- Forensic value: Application execution/installation evidence with SHA1 hashes
-- NOTE: Execution must be corroborated with additional artifacts

SELECT
  *,
  datetime(timestamp, 'unixepoch') AS record_time
FROM elastic_amcache_applications_view
WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND name != ''
ORDER BY timestamp DESC;

-- Amcache Driver Forensics: Driver binary inventory from Amcache
-- Source: elastic_amcache_driver_binary (Root\InventoryDriverBinary)
-- Forensic value: Identifies unsigned, third-party, and kernel-mode drivers
-- Use case: Rootkit detection, BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver), unauthorized drivers

SELECT
  *,
  datetime(timestamp, 'unixepoch') AS record_time,
  CASE
    WHEN driver_signed = '1' THEN 'true'
    ELSE 'false'
  END AS is_signed
FROM elastic_amcache_driver_binary
ORDER BY
  CASE WHEN driver_signed != '1' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
  CASE WHEN driver_is_kernel_mode = '1' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
  CASE WHEN driver_in_box != '1' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
  timestamp DESC;

-- Amcache Application Shortcuts: Start Menu shortcut inventory
-- Source: elastic_amcache_application_shortcut (Root\InventoryApplicationShortcut)
-- Forensic value: Tracks LNK files for execution evidence and persistence analysis

SELECT
  *,
  datetime(timestamp, 'unixepoch') AS record_time
FROM elastic_amcache_application_shortcut
WHERE shortcut_path IS NOT NULL AND shortcut_path != ''
ORDER BY timestamp DESC;

-- Amcache Device PnP: Plug and Play device history
-- Source: elastic_amcache_device_pnp (Root\InventoryDevicePnp)
-- Forensic value: USB, Bluetooth, and peripheral device connection history
-- Use case: Unauthorized device detection, data exfiltration investigation

SELECT
  *,
  datetime(timestamp, 'unixepoch') AS record_time
FROM elastic_amcache_device_pnp
ORDER BY timestamp DESC;

-- Amcache Driver Packages: Driver package metadata from Amcache
-- Source: elastic_amcache_driver_package (Root\InventoryDriverPackage)
-- Forensic value: Driver provenance, package class, provider, associated hardware IDs

SELECT
  *,
  datetime(timestamp, 'unixepoch') AS record_time
FROM elastic_amcache_driver_package
ORDER BY timestamp DESC;

-- Amcache Application Enriched: App inventory + file details + shortcuts
-- Source: elastic_amcache_applications_view + elastic_amcache_application_shortcut
-- Join: program_id = shortcut_program_id
-- Forensic value: Full application forensics with shortcut launch points
-- NOTE: Execution must be corroborated with additional artifacts

SELECT
  av.*,
  s.shortcut_path,
  s.shortcut_target_path,
  s.shortcut_aumid,
  datetime(av.timestamp, 'unixepoch') AS record_time
FROM elastic_amcache_applications_view AS av
LEFT JOIN elastic_amcache_application_shortcut AS s
  ON av.program_id = s.shortcut_program_id
WHERE av.name IS NOT NULL AND av.name != ''
ORDER BY av.timestamp DESC;

-- Amcache Driver Enriched: Driver binaries + PnP device history
-- Source: elastic_amcache_driver_binary + elastic_amcache_device_pnp
-- Join: service name (runtime binding between driver and device)
-- Forensic value: Which physical devices use each driver binary
-- Note: device columns prefixed with device_ to avoid collisions with driver columns

SELECT
  db.*,
  dp.timestamp AS device_timestamp,
  dp.date_time AS device_date_time,
  dp.model AS device_model,
  dp.manufacturer AS device_manufacturer,
  dp.description AS device_description,
  dp.driver_name AS device_driver_name,
  dp.class AS device_class,
  dp.class_guid AS device_class_guid,
  dp.enumerator AS device_enumerator,
  dp.service AS device_service,
  dp.install_state AS device_install_state,
  dp.device_state,
  dp.inf AS device_inf,
  dp.driver_ver_date,
  dp.install_date AS device_install_date,
  dp.first_install_date AS device_first_install_date,
  dp.driver_package_strong_name AS device_driver_package,
  dp.driver_ver_version AS device_driver_version,
  dp.container_id AS device_container_id,
  dp.problem_code AS device_problem_code,
  dp.provider AS device_provider,
  dp.driver_id AS device_driver_id,
  dp.bus_reported_description AS device_bus_description,
  dp.hw_id AS device_hw_id,
  dp.extended_infs AS device_extended_infs,
  dp.compid AS device_compid,
  dp.stack_id AS device_stack_id,
  dp.upper_class_filters AS device_upper_class_filters,
  dp.lower_class_filters AS device_lower_class_filters,
  dp.upper_filters AS device_upper_filters,
  dp.lower_filters AS device_lower_filters,
  dp.device_interface_classes,
  dp.location_paths AS device_location_paths,
  dp.parent_id AS device_parent_id,
  dp.matching_id AS device_matching_id,
  datetime(db.timestamp, 'unixepoch') AS record_time,
  CASE WHEN db.driver_signed = '1' THEN 'true' ELSE 'false' END AS is_signed
FROM elastic_amcache_driver_binary AS db
LEFT JOIN elastic_amcache_device_pnp AS dp
  ON db.service = dp.service
  AND dp.service != ''
ORDER BY
  CASE WHEN db.driver_signed != '1' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
  CASE WHEN db.driver_is_kernel_mode = '1' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
  CASE WHEN db.driver_in_box != '1' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
  db.timestamp DESC;

-- Windows Jump Lists Forensics with Suspicious Pattern Detection
-- Source: elastic_jumplists extension table (Elastic Agent v9.3.0+ with osquerybeat)
-- Focus: User activity timeline, lateral movement indicators (DestList hostname/MAC),
--        LOLBin execution, suspicious arguments, remote access tool usage
-- Coverage: BOTH automatic (DestList metadata) AND custom (application-defined) destination types
-- NOTE: Timestamps from elastic_jumplists are pre-formatted TEXT strings; do NOT apply datetime() conversion

SELECT
    -- Jump List identity and source
    jl.application_id,
    jl.application_name,
    jl.jumplist_type,
    jl.source_file_path,

    -- User context
    jl.username,
    jl.sid AS user_sid,

    -- DestList metadata (automatic destinations only)
    -- These fields expose lateral movement: remote hostnames and MACs differ from local host
    jl.hostname,
    jl.mac_address,
    jl.entry_number,
    jl.interaction_count,
    jl.is_pinned,
    jl.last_modified_time,

    -- Destination path (raw and GUID-resolved)
    jl.dest_entry_path,
    jl.dest_entry_path_resolved,

    -- LNK target metadata
    jl.local_path,
    jl.file_size,
    jl.command_line_arguments,
    jl.hot_key,
    jl.show_window,
    jl.icon_location,
    jl.icon_index,
    jl.name,

    -- Target timestamps (pre-formatted TEXT from extension, no conversion needed)
    jl.target_creation_time,
    jl.target_modification_time,
    jl.target_last_accessed_time,
    jl.creation_time,

    -- Volume information
    jl.volume_serial_number,
    jl.volume_type,
    jl.volume_label,

    -- VirusTotal pivot link for application_id investigation
    concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/search/', jl.application_id) AS vt_appid_link,

    -- Suspicious indicator: LOLBin detected in local_path, dest_entry_path_resolved, OR application_name
    -- Checks all three fields because Jump Lists may store the .lnk shortcut path in local_path
    -- rather than the resolved target executable
    CASE
        WHEN lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\cmd.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\powershell.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\cscript.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\wscript.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\rundll32.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\regsvr32.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\mshta.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\wmic.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\certutil.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\bitsadmin.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\msiexec.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\cmd.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\powershell.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\powershell_ise.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\pwsh.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\cscript.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\wscript.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\rundll32.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\regsvr32.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\mshta.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\wmic.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\certutil.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\bitsadmin.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\msiexec.exe'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%cmd%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%powershell%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%command prompt%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%certutil%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%bitsadmin%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%mshta%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%wmic%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%wscript%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%cscript%'
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS risky_executable_flag,

    -- Suspicious indicator: Malicious patterns in command-line arguments or shortcut name
    -- Covers encoded commands, download cradles, execution policy bypasses, hidden windows
    -- Also checks local_path for .lnk filenames containing LOLBin names (Jump Lists may
    -- store the shortcut path instead of the resolved target)
    CASE
        WHEN lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%-enc %'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%-encodedcommand %'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%-nop %'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%-noprofile%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%-w hidden%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%-windowstyle hidden%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%invoke-webrequest%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%iwr %'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%invoke-expression%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%iex(%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%iex %'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%.downloadstring(%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%.downloadfile(%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%certutil%urlcache%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%certutil%-f%'
            OR lower(jl.command_line_arguments) LIKE '%bitsadmin%transfer%'
            OR jl.command_line_arguments LIKE '%\\\\%'
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS suspicious_arguments_flag,

    -- Network indicator: DestList metadata reveals cross-host activity, or UNC target path
    -- A non-empty hostname/MAC in DestList means the shortcut was accessed from/on a remote machine
    CASE
        WHEN (jl.hostname IS NOT NULL AND jl.hostname != '')
            OR (jl.mac_address IS NOT NULL AND jl.mac_address != '')
            OR jl.local_path LIKE '\\\\%'
            OR jl.dest_entry_path LIKE '\\\\%'
            OR jl.dest_entry_path_resolved LIKE '\\\\%'
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS network_indicator_flag,

    -- Remote access tool indicator: application is a remote access / admin tool
    -- Checks application_name, local_path, AND dest_entry_path_resolved since Jump Lists
    -- may store the shortcut path in local_path instead of the resolved executable
    CASE
        WHEN lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%remote desktop%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%mstsc%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%vnc%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%teamviewer%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%anydesk%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%putty%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%winscp%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%openssh%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%mremoteng%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%realvnc%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%ultravnc%'
            OR lower(jl.application_name) LIKE '%tightvnc%'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\mstsc.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\putty.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\winscp.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\teamviewer.exe'
            OR lower(jl.local_path) LIKE '%\anydesk.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\mstsc.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\putty.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\winscp.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\teamviewer.exe'
            OR lower(jl.dest_entry_path_resolved) LIKE '%\anydesk.exe'
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS remote_access_flag

FROM elastic_jumplists AS jl

ORDER BY
    -- Pinned items first: these are deliberate user choices, relevant for persistence awareness
    jl.is_pinned DESC,
    -- Then by usage frequency: high interaction count indicates habitual/automated access
    jl.interaction_count DESC,
    -- Then by most recently modified: freshest entries are most forensically relevant
    jl.last_modified_time DESC;
